summary: Commander-004 launched all 50 squad leads on branch havoc-swarm/run-20260430-180646/commander-004, but this bundle is partial because the proof layer never reached a clean 250/250 success trail. The reconciled ledger shows 50/50 squad-lead launches and evidence for all 250 workers across launch_started, completed, and atom files, yet only 247 worker atom files exist, one squad completion event (sq-045) had to be reconciled after the fact, and concurrent child writes clobbered swarm-state counters. Across 50 squad reports (37 success, 13 partial), the dominant findings were: premium/banned-model and SS-250 success conditions are still mostly prompt/metadata enforced; monitor validation ignores child-agents.jsonl and mutable swarm-state can drift; collaboration-bus schema/adoption docs are weak; and stop/recovery/CI coverage remains incomplete, including false-positive metaswarm smoke coverage. Adopted consensus aligns with commander-001 blockers, commander-002 collaboration hold, commander-005 synthesis-readiness gaps, and commander-004 telemetry/runtime-gate findings.

notes: Telemetry proof is not success-grade until child-agents.jsonl is validated and swarm-state is reducer- or lock-backed.
Premium-model compliance and SS-250 success conditions still need hard runtime gates, not prompt-only enforcement.
Collaboration-bus, stop/recovery, and CI readiness gaps keep the pre-build QA verdict at partial.
